alkylation reagent: alkylated with iodoacetamide (10 mM) for 15 min at room temperature (RT) in the dark.
alkylation concentration: alkylated with iodoacetamide (10 mM) for 15 min at room temperature (RT) in the dark.
cleavage agent: digested by trypsin and these digests were further processed and lyophilized.
collision energy: CID scans were acquired in a linear trap quadrupole (LTQ) with 35% normalized collision energy (NCE).
fragmentation method: collision-induced dissociation (CID) MS/MS spectra in ion trap.
reduction reagent: Extracts were reduced with 4.5 mM DTT for 30 min at 55 °C.
reduction concentration: Extracts were reduced with 4.5 mM DTT for 30 min at 55 °C.
